perturbation.original_text: Recipients will not disclose, distribute, copy or use any Confidential Information or the fact that a Transaction is being considered or is under a production contract without the prior written consent of the other Party and then only to the extent specified in such consent.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipients will not disclose, distribute, copy or use any Public Information or the fact that a Transaction is being considered or is under a production contract without the prior written consent of the other Party and then only to the extent specified in such consent.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Public Information' in Section 2, it contradicts the rest of the document which is based on protecting 'Confidential Information', also it creates a contradiction with the term it self in the text since a Public Information doesn't require permission for it to be disclosed, distributed or copied. Now recipients are restricted on what they can do to 'Public Information,' blurring the lines of whether information should be kept secret.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Use and Non-Disclosure. Recipients will not disclose, distribute, copy or use any Public Information or the fact that a Transaction is being considered or is under a production contract without the prior written consent of the other Party and then only to the extent specified in such consent. Confidential Information may be used only to the extent reasonably required for the purposes of evaluating, producing or carrying out the Transaction; provided however, that Recipients may only disclose the Confidential Information to those of its contractors, vendors, directors, officers, advisors, agents and employees ("Representatives") who need to know such Confidential Information for the purpose of evaluating, performing or carrying out or producing the Transaction and who shall be bound by a written agreement or other legally enforceable written obligations to preserve the confidentiality of the Confidential Information in a manner consistent with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Public Information'. The explanation states this creates a contradiction because Public Information, by definition, doesn't require permission for disclosure, distribution, or copying. The contradicted text further exemplifies this by restricting actions on 'Public Information' in a way that is illogical and conflicts with the intended protection of confidential data, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition because the change makes obligations conflicting, leading to uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: Without limiting the foregoing, where the Discloser is the Producer and a Recipient Representative is a cost consultant of the Client or Agency, the Recipient shall secure its Representative’s express written agreement that it will not make any business or other use of, and will in perpetuity hold in confidence, all Confidential Information acquired or learned by such Representative and shall not use or disclose such information except as permitted in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Without limiting the foregoing, where the Discloser is the Producer and a Recipient Representative is a cost consultant of the Client or Agency, the Recipient shall secure its Representative’s express written agreement that it will not make any business or other use of, and will in perpetuity hold in confidence, all Public Information acquired or learned by such Representative and shall not use or disclose such information except as permitted in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Public Information' creates an in-textual contradiction. It's nonsensical to require someone to perpetually hold 'Public Information' in confidence, as that is antithetical to its nature. This introduces confusion and ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by it or its Representatives, whether or not such Representatives are associated with the Discloser at the time of the breach. Without limiting the foregoing, where the Discloser is the Producer and a Recipient Representative is a cost consultant of the Client or Agency, the Recipient shall secure its Representative’s express written agreement that it will not make any business or other use of, and will in perpetuity hold in confidence, all Public Information acquired or learned by such Representative and shall not use or disclose such information except as permitted in this Agreement. Prohibited uses and disclosures by such Representative include, but are not limited to, uses for or disclosures to a competitor of the Producer and uses for or disclosures to any other client or prospective client of such Representative, regardless of whether the Confidential Information is aggregated with other information or its not identified with the Producer.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Public Information' in the Changed Text directly contradicts the expectation in the Contradicted Text that the Recipient's Representative will hold the information in confidence. 'Public Information' by definition is not held in confidence, creating a clear and strong in-text contradiction. The Contradicted Text specifies that the Recipient will be responsible for ensuring the Representative holds certain information acquired in confidence. The Changed Text requires the Representative to hold 'Public Information' in confidence, which is nonsensical and therefore unenforceable, thus creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations of confidentiality in this Agreement do not extend to any item of Confidential Information which (i) is publicly known at the time of its disclosure, (ii) is lawfully received by a Recipient from a third party not known to the Recipient to be under a similar confidential agreement to this Agreement or under obligation of confidentiality to the Discloser, (iii) is published or otherwise made known to the public by the Discloser of Confidential Information, (iv) was generated independently by the receiving Recipient without reliance on Confidential Information (as proven by supporting documentation evidencing such independent 428249v2 generation) or (v) was received either prior to the Effective Date of this Agreement or was received for the first time only after the termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of confidentiality in this Agreement do not extend to any item of Confidential Information which (i) is not publicly known at the time of its disclosure, (ii) is lawfully received by a Recipient from a third party not known to the Recipient to be under a similar confidential agreement to this Agreement or under obligation of confidentiality to the Discloser, (iii) is published or otherwise made known to the public by the Discloser of Confidential Information, (iv) was generated independently by the receiving Recipient without reliance on Confidential Information (as proven by supporting documentation evidencing such independent 428249v2 generation) or (v) was received either prior to the Effective Date of this Agreement or was received for the first time only after the termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'is publicly known' to 'is not publicly known' directly contradicts the purpose of the clause, which is to exclude publicly known information from confidentiality obligations. Now, the agreement implies that the obligations *do* extend to information that isn't publicly known which creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The obligations of confidentiality in this Agreement do not extend to any item of Confidential Information which (i) is not publicly known at the time of its disclosure, (ii) is lawfully received by a Recipient from a third party not known to the Recipient to be under a similar confidential agreement to this Agreement or under obligation of confidentiality to the Discloser, (iii) is published or otherwise made known to the public by the Discloser of Confidential Information, (iv) was generated independently by the receiving Recipient without reliance on Confidential Information (as proven by supporting documentation evidencing such independent 428249v2 generation) or (v) was received either prior to the Effective Date of this Agreement or was received for the first time only after the termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of Confidential Information by stating that the obligations of confidentiality *do* extend to information that is *not* publicly known. This directly contradicts the intention of the original text which states obligations of confidentiality do not extend to information which *is* publicly known. This creates a strong, in-text contradiction because the contradicted text is the same as the changed text, creating a clear conflict in the definition of confidential information and thus it would cause uncertainty. This aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction as the change in 'is publicly known' to 'is not publicly known' uses the term 'Confidential Information' in a conflicting manner.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees that it shall take reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure and unauthorized use of the Confidential Information. Without limiting the foregoing, Recipient shall take at least those measures that it takes to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature, but in no event less than reasonable measures. Recipient shall reproduce Discloser's proprietary rights notices on any copies, in the same manner in which such notices were set forth in or on the original. Recipient shall immediately notify Discloser upon discovery of any loss or unauthorized disclosure of any Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient agrees that it shall take minimal measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure and unauthorized use of the Confidential Information. Without limiting the foregoing, Recipient shall take at least those measures that it takes to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature, but in no event less than reasonable measures. Recipient shall reproduce Discloser's proprietary rights notices on any copies, in the same manner in which such notices were set forth in or on the original. Recipient shall immediately notify Discloser upon discovery of any loss or unauthorized disclosure of any Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'reasonable measures' to 'minimal measures' creates an in-text contradiction, because later in the same sentence the clause establishes that measures should be reasonable. This makes the level of care required for protection ambiguous and difficult to enforce. Recipient is taking minimal measures while at the same time, they should take the reasonable measures.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees that it shall take minimal measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure and unauthorized use of the Confidential Information. Without limiting the foregoing, Recipient shall take at least those measures that it takes to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature, but in no event less than reasonable measures. Recipient shall reproduce Discloser's proprietary rights notices on any copies, in the same manner in which such notices were set forth in or on the original. Recipient shall immediately notify Discloser upon discovery of any loss or unauthorized disclosure of any Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'reasonable measures' to 'minimal measures' while the subsequent clause states 'but in no event less than reasonable measures.' This creates an in-text contradiction because Recipient is asked to take both minimal and reasonable measures, which are conflicting levels of action. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text in this instance, highlighting that the contradiction exists within the same section due to the alteration. As the explanation states, this creates an ambiguity in the contract and would make enforcement unclear.
